names.thesis: Dominant global cell maker whose own Jianxiawo lepidolite restart is the bearish swing factor for lithium — as a metals Consumer it benefits from cheaper carbonate inputs (om-linkage 6/10, most lithium cost passed through with upstream buffers). Stock is -14.6% vs 50DMA amid the China EV price war and A-share lithium unwind, so the commodity tailwind is offset by weak tape and demand-side margin pressure. Neutral: quality franchise, two-sided lithium backdrop, no momentum.
risks: China EV price war compressing cell ASPs

evidence.note: CATL Jianxiawo lithium mine resumes production; carbonate market enters 'rebalancing', futures broke below CNY150K/t cost line
CATL mine restart + West Africa's largest lithium mine startup cited as supply signals suppressing lithium-sector valuations
score 6/10 — Most lithium cost passed through; upstream buffers

names.thesis: H1-26 NEV sales fell 15.7% YoY and the stock is -16% YTD, but June inflected (+5.5% YoY, 403K units), Q2 volume +58% QoQ with Deutsche Bank modelling net profit +145% QoQ to RMB10B, and exports (~half of June sales, 1.5M target) plus UK registrations +95% are doing the heavy lifting. Vertically integrated LFP means weak metals coupling (om 4/10) — this is an EV volume/margin story, not a commodity trade. Neutral-to-constructive: profit inflection vs domestic price war.
risks: China price war and H1 BEV sales -15% YoY
weak commodity linkage limits fit vs mandate

analyst.note: Simply Wall St 'modestly undervalued'; Deutsche Bank Q2 NP +145% QoQ forecast

evidence.note: June sales 403,472 (+5.5% YoY); Q2 volume +58% QoQ to 1.1M; DB forecasts Q2 net profit RMB10B (+145% QoQ)
H1-26 NEV sales -15.7% YoY to 1.81M; BEV H1 -15.2%; still reclaimed global pure-EV lead from Tesla
UK H1 registrations +95% YoY to 37,995; 8.74% UK plug-in share; exports ~half of June sales
score 4/10 — Vertically integrated LFP; low Bigdata signal

names.thesis: Q2 preliminary operating profit missed hard (-77% YoY to ₩113B vs ₩249B consensus) on weak US EV demand, but the quarter turned QoQ profitable on +15.3% sales and the street (Shinhan, NH) frames H2 as an ESS-led rebound (ESS revenue +46% H2 vs H1, DTE 6GWh contract) with FY revenue guidance of +15-20% maintained. Strongest metal linkage of the cell makers (om 9/10, ASP tracks Li/Ni with 1-2 month lag), so any lithium firming into Q3 restock is a revenue tailwind. Stock -19% vs 50DMA already discounts much of the miss.
catalysts: H2 ESS ramp (DTE 6GWh; Ohio/Honda storage output started)
risks: US EV demand slump / GM truck program delay
Q2 miss magnitude (-77% YoY vs consensus)

analyst.note: Shinhan: ESS entering new growth phase, H2 ESS rev +46% vs H1; NH: broader H2 recovery; FY rev guidance +15-20% maintained

evidence.note: Q2 operating profit guidance -77% YoY to ₩113B vs LSEG SmartEstimate ₩249B; weak EV demand, GM delays

Q2 prelim sales ₩7.56T (+15.3% QoQ), OP ₩113.3B — read as 'signal of rebound' for Korean batteries

names.thesis: The most contested Korean battery name: DB Securities sees Q2 back to break-even on ESS tariff refunds and North American demand (PT ₩840K) while LS Securities flags prismatic share loss (PT ₩419K) — a 2x spread in targets around a stock -24% vs 50DMA. AI data-center BBU demand (cylindrical cells via Simplo/Trend Power, high utilization) and Hyundai IONIQ 3/EV2 wins are genuine H2 catalysts, but cost pass-through caps metal linkage (om 5/10). Neutral: crushed valuation, earnings floor forming, EV core still shrinking.
catalysts: Q2 earnings: BEP/surplus swing on ESS tariff refund
Hyundai/Kia IONIQ 3 & EV2 supply from H2
risks: EV battery sales decline and prismatic market-share erosion

analyst.note: Split street: DB Buy PT ₩840K vs LS ₩419K; Daol cut PT ₩900K→₩770K but sees buying opportunity from Q2 earnings season

Supplies AI data-center BBU cells via Taiwan's Simplo; cylindrical production rising, utilization high, supply short

names.thesis: Best momentum in the group (+15.6% vs 50DMA, +69.7% vs 200DMA, record market cap) because Panasonic has repositioned from EV-battery laggard to AI data-center power play: ¥350B of AI-infra capex to the Energy unit, Kansas EV plant repurposed to data-center cells by FY28, and a ~¥1T data-center ESS sales target management calls a 'minimum commitment' (>20% ROIC goal). Battery-metals linkage is weak (om 4/10 — group dilutes Li/Co exposure), so it scores as a structural long on AI energy demand rather than a metals trade; chase risk after the double is the main caveat.
catalysts: Kansas data-center cell production (FY2028) and third Mexico plant
risks: stock doubled YTD — expectations now high
US EV battery demand weakness (Tesla exposure)
weak metals coupling = low fit to commodity book

Market cap at record high, stock roughly doubled YTD on 'AI compute water-seller' repositioning; energy business targets ¥2T sales, >¥300B adj. OP

names.thesis: Q1-26 swung to HK$1.84B net profit (+616% YoY) on recovered lithium prices and JPMorgan just upgraded to Buy (PT HK$70) with Citi at Buy HK$78 — yet the stock has been hammered -36% vs 50DMA in the July lithium unwind triggered by CATL's Jianxiawo restart. Management argues fundamentals are still in tight balance and the June price break sets up peak-season restocking, but 2025 ex-items was loss-making and Citi notes battery-segment margin deterioration offsets lithium upside (om 8/10). Two-sided per the CBC 'avoid stockpiling pre-Jianxiawo' call: quality leveraged lithium beta for a bounce, not yet a trend long.
catalysts: peak-season (Q3) downstream restocking Ganfeng itself flags
DoD $300M lithium stockpile tender supporting ex-China pricing
risks: battery segment margin drag (1% share in NEV logistics batteries)

analyst.consensus: Moderate Buy / Hold (TipRanks via Business Insider)
analyst.priceTarget: HK$77.4 avg; JPM Buy HK$70 (Jul 9), Citi Buy HK$78.12 (Jul 5)

evidence.note: J.P. Morgan upgrade to Buy, PT HK$70; consensus Moderate Buy, avg PT HK$77.37; Q1 rev HK$9.2B, NP HK$1.84B vs loss LY
Q1-26 net profit +616% YoY, but 2025 ex-non-recurring was negative; Citi: lithium price gains partly offset by battery margin deterioration
Ganfeng: lithium supply-demand still tight balance; June drop = off-season + inventory fears; sets up peak-season procurement

names.thesis: Pure-play lithium (om 8/10) with Greenbushes-discounted monthly concentrate pricing, riding the same H1 earnings surge as peers — but the stock fell ~19-31% in days as the Jianxiawo restart flipped supply expectations, and a CNY6B two-year bond program raised leverage questions mid-selloff. Institutions are 'voting with their feet' even as H1 pre-announcements are strong: classic earnings-realized / cycle-peaking setup in a NEUTRAL lithium tape. Hold off until carbonate stabilizes above the CNY150K/t mica cost line.
catalysts: H1 results (lithium names pre-announcing large increases)

evidence.note: Registered CNY6B two-year bond program; shares -7% Shenzhen / -6% HK that morning
Lithium stocks: strong earnings but capital exiting; CATL mine restart + West Africa startup suppressing valuations; Tianqi -19% in three days
Concentrate purchases priced monthly off four agency quotes with shareholder discount; ops/sales normal, unaware of production-cut rumors
score 8/10 — Revenue dropped RMB27B on selling-price decline; pure lithium

names.thesis: Highest-linkage cathode pure-play (om 9/10 — NRV writedowns reverse on metal price rises) trading -31.8% vs 50DMA, squarely in the 'Korean cathode names crushed -40..50%' bucket. The ₩1.2T rights offering (June 30) to fund Indonesian nickel smelting is strategically sound (cost-advantaged high-nickel NCM) but dilutive into a weak tape, and the EV cathode demand engine is still soft. Q3 restock would mechanically help via NRV reversals; until then it is a beaten-up beta without a trigger.
catalysts: Q3 restock → NRV reversal on Li/Ni upticks
Indonesia nickel integration (cost advantage in NCM)
risks: EV cathode demand and Korean sector de-rating
high-nickel NCM losing share to LFP

evidence.note: 10th anniversary: sales ₩2.5T in 2025; Debrecen Hungary 54kt plant complete; Indonesia nickel expansion funded by ₩1.2T rights offering announced Jun 30
Holdco EcoPro to subscribe >120% of allocation to limit dilution concern; CEO: securing early position in nickel for NCM cost leadership
score 9/10 — Ni smelter spread widens on higher LME; Li tailwind

names.thesis: Korea's only integrated cathode+anode producer (om 9/10 — every deck tracks Li/Ni/Co as the input-output driver) is -28.8% vs 50DMA with momentum at zero, fully participating in the Korean cathode wreck. Strategic positioning improved at the margin — Factorial solid-state MoU/investment (Dec-25/Jan-26) and a locked lithium/nickel/graphite raw-material chain via POSCO Group — and its graphite anode line is the main non-China alternative, a call option on anode-supply-chain de-risking. Needs the Q3 restock or a Li/Ni uptick to work; until then dead money with balance-sheet backing.
catalysts: US/EU non-China graphite anode sourcing decisions
risks: EV cathode oversupply and Korean sector de-rating
China graphite oversupply capping anode economics

evidence.note: POSCO Future M MoU (Dec-25) + investment (Jan-26) for all-solid-state battery materials; only Korean producer of both cathode and anode; robust Li/Ni/graphite supply chain

names.thesis: World's largest cobalt refiner with an integrated DRC-Indonesia-cathode chain, screening cheapest in its cohort (~12x rolling P/E) with >33% share in 9-series ultra-high-nickel cathode and institutional targets >50% above spot — but the stock has round-tripped (-24% vs 50DMA, -34.6% vs 200DMA) as the July battery-chain unwind erased a June cobalt-driven pop. The DRC quota/stockpiling story tightens cobalt long-term while high downstream inventories block near-term pass-through (backdrop NEUTRAL, sulfate €11.2/kg flat), and cobalt is only ~6% of revenue vs nickel ~46% combined. Relative-value long on any cobalt price inflection; om 8/10.
catalysts: DRC export quotas/strategic stockpiling tightening cobalt
cobalt sulfate price inflection as domestic inventories deplete
ternary shipments >100kt run-rate; ultra-high-Ni share gains
risks: downstream inventories too high for cobalt pass-through
A-share battery-metals de-rating momentum (0.0 momentum sub)

evidence.note: DRC (75% of world cobalt) tightening export quotas + strategic stockpiling; domestic inventories depleting, price-rise expectations building; integrated names like Huayou fully benefit
Rolling P/E 12.07x lowest in screen; 2025E ternary cathode shipments >100kt; 9-series ultra-high-Ni share >33%, power cylindrical ternary >60%; >50% upside to consensus target
Revenue mix: nickel products 32% + Ni intermediates 14.5%, cathode 18.5%, cobalt 6.2%, precursors 5.5%, lithium 4.3% — diversified, cobalt is the label not the bulk

names.thesis: Australia's flagship pure-play spodumene producer (om 9/10 — every price move flows to the bottom line) guiding to higher FY27 production at lower unit costs after completing the Pilgangoora expansion, with an idle circuit as free optionality and a new mid-stream facility commissioning. The -21.3% vs 50DMA pullback maps exactly to the Jianxiawo-restart supply scare, yet spodumene held up better than China chemicals — physical concentrate market still tight. Best-quality way to own the lithium NEUTRAL-two-sided call; buy weakness only if carbonate stabilizes.
catalysts: July quarterly: shipped volumes, realised spodumene price, unit costs
possible restart of idled processing circuit
Q3 restock / DoD stockpile tender supporting sentiment
risks: spodumene-carbonate spread normalization compressing realized prices

evidence.note: Guides stronger spodumene output with continued unit-cost declines; Pilgangoora expansion complete; idle circuit under restart review

names.thesis: Diversified miner where lithium is only one of three engines (om 6/10 — iron ore and mining services dilute the linkage), so it under-captures any lithium upside while keeping full exposure to its own leverage and governance overhang; the stock is -11.4% vs 50DMA with composite 51.6. Bigdata search returned no fresh June-July coverage — no visible catalyst. In a NEUTRAL two-sided lithium tape, prefer the pure-plays (PLS) for beta and skip the conglomerate; balance-sheet news remains the swing factor.

risks: iron ore softness compounding lithium volatility

names.thesis: Pure-play precursor (pCAM) and battery-recycling leader (om 7/10) with a real policy tailwind — China's 15th Five-Year circular-economy plan showcases GEM by name, and Q1 power-battery recycling volumes grew 40% YoY on +5% revenue. But the stock sits at 5.7% of its 52-week range (-20.9% vs 200DMA) and a partner (Vital/Bluescope Lithium Core) walked away from the Indonesian nickel project capital raise in Feb-26, a small credibility ding. Neutral: policy-supported recycling growth vs precursor margin compression from the cathode glut.
catalysts: cobalt/nickel price recovery lifting recycled-metal spreads
